authoritative_sources.title: EDI v5010 852 INBOUND Product Activity
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Macy's official vendor EDI specification is named-industry operating guidance for the product-activity records retailers actually exchange with vendors, including sold, returned, on-hand, and on-order data tied to item identifiers used in apparel analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, channel reporting intake, and cross-system reconciliation
used_for: sold, returned, on-hand, and on-order metric lineage

authoritative_sources.title: Product Safety and Quality Assurance Tools and Processes
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Target's official retailer guidance is named-industry operating guidance showing how apparel and general-merchandise workflows rely on approval samples, third-party testing, label validation, record keeping, and recall handling before stronger claims are made.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence sufficiency review and disclosure boundary checking
used_for: refusal when product-safety posture is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Apparel and General Merchandise E-Commerce Fulfillment Guideline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is a recognized standards body for apparel and general merchandise trade-item identification and fulfillment workflows. This guideline ties GTIN, color, and size identifiers to inventory visibility, selling trends, and fulfillment accuracy.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, item-grain normalization, and fulfillment-aware reconciliation

authoritative_sources.title: Color and Size Codes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US color and size code guidance is a recognized workflow reference for normalizing apparel and footwear size and color attributes so analytics preserves the right variant grain.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: industry terminology normalization and item-master validation

authoritative_sources.title: IFRS S2 Climate-related Disclosures - Industry-based Guidance, Consumer Goods Appendix B8 Apparel, Accessories & Footwear
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IFRS Foundation is the official publisher of ISSB standards. Appendix B8 provides apparel, accessories, and footwear-specific disclosure topics and metric categories such as raw-material sourcing, supplier tiers, and manufacturing emissions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis and sustainability disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The SEC MD&A final rule is the authoritative public baseline for KPI contextualization, metric-definition transparency, and trend explanation when apparel analytics feeds board, lender, or investor-style reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: analysis package assembly and executive disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is the primary U.S. evidence standard for care labels. It defines durable-label expectations and the requirement for a reliable basis behind care instructions, which limits analytics claims about quality or compliance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence sufficiency review and unsupported-claim refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Clothing and Textiles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's clothing and textiles business guidance is the public baseline for textile, wool, and apparel label fields such as identity, fiber content, and country of origin that affect product-claim and disclosure boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: label-content evidence review and boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Clothing Business Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. product-safety regulator for clothing. Its clothing guidance defines flammability, children's-product, testing, certification, and tracking-label expectations that can materially change apparel analytics conclusions and disclosures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: children's-product boundary review and evidence gap handling
used_for: escalation when age-grade facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Tracking Labels
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's tracking-label FAQ is authoritative public guidance for required traceability fields on children's products, which affects evidence sufficiency for kid's apparel and footwear requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability-aware evidence review and launch-boundary escalation
used_for: refusal when launch claims exceed record completeness

workflow_stages.name: request intake and apparel prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Normalize brand, retailer or channel program, destination market, age grade, product family, season, style-color-size grain, audience, and source-precedence context before analysis starts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: item-master, sales, inventory, and returns record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate PLM, PIM, GTIN or UPC, POS, retailer product-activity, ERP, WMS, OMS, returns, and exception records to determine which system is authoritative for the requested metric.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and denominator control
workflow_stages.description: Define sell-through, return rate, weeks of supply, markdown penetration, size-curve performance, certified-material percentages, supplier-tier counts, and similar metrics while preserving channel, variant, age-grade, and disclosure context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: cross-system reconciliation and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile retailer, POS, inventory, fulfillment, returns, compliance, and sustainability records without silently allowing a dashboard or presentation deck to override the operational source of truth.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: analysis package assembly and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, narrative summary, confidence posture, and action thresholds with explicit apparel evidence lineage, then stop when compliance, launch, sourcing, pricing, or external disclosure authority would be required.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: PLM or PDM style masters, season line plans, assortment files, style-color-size matrices, and revision logs
GTIN or UPC variant records, size curves, color codes, footwear sizing references, and retailer item setup files
POS, ecommerce, OMS, ERP, WMS, and retailer 852 product-activity records for sold, returned, on-hand, on-order, reserve, and in-transit states
markdown calendars, promotional calendars, pricing ladders, allocation rules, and inventory aging or weeks-of-supply reports
return-reason codes, complaint logs, fit or quality exception reports, and defect or damage summaries
care-label basis evidence, fiber-content and origin fields, children's-product test reports, certification records, and tracking-label fields
supplier-tier workbooks, certified-material logs, manufacturing emissions workbooks, board-deck drafts, and disclosure review notes
systems_of_record: PLM or product data management system

allowed: Classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis.
Prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and threshold summaries without overstating authority.
escalate: required brand, retailer, market, age-grade, variant, or source records are missing and would change the outcome
public source guidance, tenant policy, and operating practice conflict materially about metric logic, evidence sufficiency, or disclosure posture
the request raises pricing, assortment, technical-design, sourcing, compliance, legal, finance-signoff, or external disclosure questions outside the lane
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
treat a dashboard, screenshot, or slide as sufficient when PLM, retailer, POS, ERP, WMS, or compliance records materially disagree
approve launch, pricing, markdown, sourcing, compliance, or disclosure decisions because trends appear favorable
state unsupported causal, benchmark, compliance, sustainability, or disclosure claims

domain_failure_modes: Treating apparel analytics as generic retail BI work and failing to request style-color-size grain, retailer program, market, age-grade, or footwear-sizing context.
Collapsing style, color, size, and channel data into one denominator without recording differences in sold, received, available, reserve, markdown, or returned states.
Letting dashboards or exported summaries override PLM, GS1, retailer EDI, POS, ERP, WMS, returns, or compliance records without preserving the conflict.
Using favorable returns or sell-through trends to imply product-safety, labeling, launch, or compliance clearance.
Publishing sustainability or certified-material metrics while supplier-tier, raw-material, or emissions records remain incomplete or conflicting.
Turning descriptive apparel trends into unsupported causal, benchmark, customer-facing, investor-facing, or regulator-facing claims.
Allowing the analytics lane to widen into merchandising, pricing, technical design, sourcing, product compliance, or disclosure signoff authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Destination-market labeling and consumer-law overlays outside the U.S.
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public baseline here is anchored in U.S. FTC, CPSC, SEC, and IFRS sources plus named retailer guidance. Tenants selling into other markets must supply the destination-market labeling, product-safety, and disclosure overlay at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific margin waterfall and markdown-calendar logic
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources support metric discipline and apparel records, but exact gross-margin, markdown, and reserve-handling logic still requires tenant definitions and source precedence to avoid silent metric drift.

notes: Authoritative apparel analytics records are the tenant-designated PLM, item master, POS, OMS, ERP, WMS, returns, retailer EDI, compliance, and disclosure systems constrained by GS1, FTC, CPSC, IFRS, SEC, and named retailer baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before final merchandising, pricing, compliance, sourcing, launch, or external disclosure approval even when the analysis is commercially urgent.
